header.select.you: Play a tile from your hand
header.capture.you: Place a captured tile in your panorama
header.capture.player: {player} must place a captured tile in their panorama
header.second-chance: Second Chance: a tile is revealed
header.rainbow.you: Place a Rainbow in your panorama
header.rainbow.player: {player} places a Rainbow in their panorama
header.spirit.you: Choose a Spirit and place it in your panorama
header.spirit.player: {player} places a Spirit in their panorama
header.refill.you: You draw to complete your hand
header.deer.you: Add a tile from your hand to your panorama
header.deer.player: {player} adds a tile from their hand to their panorama
header.eagle.you: Add a tile from the draw pile to your panorama
header.eagle.player: {player} adds a tile from the draw pile to their panorama
header.dragonfly.you: You place a tile from your opponent’s hand
header.dragonfly.player: {player} places a tile drawn at random from your hand
header.dragonfly.spectator: {player} places a tile from their opponent’s hand
header.beetle.you: Add a tile from the display to your panorama
header.beetle.player: {player} adds a tile from the display to their panorama
header.evil.you: Choose one of your opponent’s Spirits to cover
header.squirrel.you: You place all the tiles from your hand in your panorama
header.squirrel.player: {player} places all the tiles from their hand in their panorama
header.fox.you: You must choose the tile {opponent} will play next turn
header.fox.player: {player} must choose the tile you will play next turn
header.fox.spectator: {player} must choose the tile {opponent} will play next turn
header.viper.you: You must take an isolated Land tile from {opponent}’s panorama
header.viper.player: {player} must take an isolated Land tile from your panorama
header.viper.spectator: {player} must take an isolated Land tile from {opponent}’s panorama
header.evil.firefly.you: You must block a location in {opponent}’s panorama
header.evil.firefly.player: {player} must block a location in your panorama
header.evil.firefly.spectator: {player} must block a location in {opponent}’s panorama
help.tile.line: This tile is in the display: to capture it, you must play a tile from your hand in the same row or column.
help.tile.panorama.you: This tile is in your panorama.
help.tile.pile: {number, plural, one{There is # tile} other{There are # tiles}} left in the draw pile.
help.tile.hand.you: This tile is in your hand.
help.tile.position: This tile goes in row {line} and column {column} of the panorama.
help.tile.fire:
This tile has a <0>Fire</0> icon <1/>.
You can only light a <0>Fire</0> if you can follow a path between the <2>tile</2> on which the <0>Fire</0> is and a <2>tile</2> in the bottom row of your panorama (the tiles with a red number box). Each <0>Fire</0> linked to the bottom row gives you 1 <3/>.
help.rainbow.joker: A Rainbow tile is a joker which can replace any tile in the panorama.
help.spirit.take: To encounter a Spirit, you need to form a square with 4 Land tiles.
help.spirit.effect: This Spirit has the following effect:
help.trout: Take a <0>Rainbow</0> <1>tile</1> and place it in your panorama.
help.deer: Add a <1>tile</1> from your hand to your panorama. You will draw 2 <1>tiles</1> at the end of your turn.
help.eagle: Add a <1>tile</1> drawn at random from the draw pile to your panorama.
help.bear: Immediately draw a <1>tile</1> (you will then have an extra tile in your hand during the game).
help.dragonfly: Add a <1>tile</1> that you’ve randomly drawn from your opponent’s hand to your panorama. Your opponent draws a new <1>tile</1> from the Land draw pile in compensation, if there are any left.
help.beetle: Add a face-up <1>tile</1> of your choice from the display to your panorama.
help.groundhog: Finish your turn, then play another full turn (so you get 2 turns).
help.bee: + 1 <2/> for every <0>Spirit</0> with a plain background in your panorama.
help.ladybug: + 1 <2/> for every <0>Fire</0> icon that has not been lit.
help.wolf: + 1 <2/> for each separate panorama section (even those made of a single <1>tile</1>).
help.squirrel: At the end of the game, before you calculate the scores, add all the remaining <1>tiles</1> in your hand to your panorama.
help.bird: + 1 <2/> for every <0>Spirit</0> with a blue background in your panorama, except this one.
help.spirit-evil:
When you draw the <0>Evil Spirit</0>, you’re forced to keep both it and the other <0>Spirit</0> you drew at the same time. Place the regular <0>Spirit</0> in your panorama as usual and keep the <0>Evil Spirit</0> somewhere in front of your panorama.
The next time you encounter a new <0>Spirit</0>, give the <0>Evil Spirit</0> to your opponent… and they can give it back to you if they encounter a <0>Spirit</0>, and so on.
<0>At the end of the game, the player with the Evil Spirit has to use it to cover one of their own Spirits, chosen by their opponent.</0>
help.spirit-evil.firefly:
<4>Firefly extension:</4> When you draw the <0>Evil Spirit</0>:

Place it in the opponent’s panorama, adjacent to a Land tile (with the same placement rules as if it were a Rainbow).
This location is now forbidden! (When the player hosting the <0>Evil Spirit</0> captures the corresponding tile, they remove it from the game.)
To get rid of the <0>Evil Spirit</0>, you’ll need to welcome a new Spirit to your panorama. Then choose a Spirit by following the basic rules, then send the <0>Evil Spirit</0> back to the opponent, adjacent to one of their Land tiles.

The <0>Evil Spirit</0> gives no penalty to the host at the end of the game.
help.spirit.timing: The effects of the <0>Spirits</0> on a plain background are triggered immediately the moment you encounter them. As for those of the <0>Spirits</0> on a blue background, they activate at the end of the game.
help.cow: + 1 <2/> bonus for each Land tile in your second largest panorama area
help.phoenix: + 1 <2/> for each of your Fireflies <1> (whether visible from the bottom or not)</1>
help.viper:
Immediately take an isolated Land tile from your opponent’s panorama and add it to your panorama.
If your opponent has no isolated tile, the effect is lost.
help.fox:
Your opponent reveals the tiles in their hand.
Choose the one they will play next turn. They then take back the tiles in their hand.
help.firefly.desc:
During the same turn, a player may make several squares in their panorama. In this situation:

- The first square will host a Spirit following the basic rules
- Each additional square will host a Firefly token

Each Firefly visible from the bottom (i.e. accessible via a tile path starting from the bottom line - red numbers) gives you +1 <star />.

The game ends immediately as soon as a player gets their 4th Firefly token. In this case, they are declared the winner without having to count the <star />.
tuto.goal: In Solstis, you need to recreate a panorama made up of these <0>Land tiles</0>.
tuto.tile: Each Land tile is unique, and goes into the panorama at a specific location, indicated by the number and associated colour.
tuto.hand: This is your hand. On your turn, you must play one of the 3 tiles in your hand in the centre of the playing area.
tuto.capture: After playing a Land tile, a <0>capture</0> takes place if this tile belongs to the same row (box of the same colour and similar landscape) OR the same column (same number) as one of the visible tiles in the display.
tuto.queue: The tile you have played allows you to capture one of these two tiles: the first because it is in the same row, the other because it is in the same column.
tuto.place: Choose one of the captured tiles and place it in your panorama.
tuto.played: The tile that allowed you to capture the other is also automatically added to your panorama.
tuto.opponent: It’s your opponent’s turn. They, too, capture a tile.
tuto.play.2: It’s your turn again. Play this tile now.
tuto.second-chance: The tile you have played does not allow you to make a capture. In this case, it joins the tiles in the display and you get a Second Chance: a tile is drawn at random from the draw pile, and this tile can trigger a capture.
tuto.place.2: The tile that has been drawn allows you to make a capture! Place the captured tile in your panorama.
tuto.spirit: When you recreate a square as is the case here, you encounter a <0>Spirit</0>! You draw <0>2 Spirit</0> tiles at random and choose one, which you place in the centre of the square.
tuto.rainbow: This Spirit allows you to place a <0>Rainbow tile</0> in your panorama. This is a joker, which you can place next to any tile. Place the Rainbow tile wherever you like.
tuto.rainbow.win: You also get a Rainbow tile if you don’t make a capture on your turn (neither with the tile you play nor with the tile you draw as a Second Chance).
tuto.turn: Players take it in turns to play until the draw pile is empty and neither player can make a capture.
tuto.scoring: At the end of the game, each <0>Land tile</0> in your largest reconstructed area scores 1 <1/>. Note that the <0>Rainbow tiles</0> participating in this area score no points.
tuto.fire: Some tiles, like this one at the top of the mountain, have a <0>Fire</0> icon: <1/>
tuto.fire.lighten: A <0>Fire</0> is only lit if a path can be made between the tile on which it is present and a tile at the bottom of the panorama (number on red box). Each <0>Fire</0> linked to the bottom row yields 1 <1/>.
tuto.spirit-score: Finally, each <0>Spirit</0> yields 1 <1/>, and some will give you other bonuses <1/>: at any time you can click on a <0>Spirit</0> tile to see its effect!
tuto.winner: The player with the most <1/> at the end of the game wins. Now it’s your turn to play - good luck!
game-over.win.firefly: You win the game with your 4 Fireflies
game-over.win.firefly.player: {player} wins the game with their 4 Fireflies
